danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to player; causes damage and energy depletion
Direct damage; loss of life if energy is depleted

danger_signals.visual_form: Transporter doors that cannot be opened
danger_signals.meaning: Blocked paths; attempting entry kills the player until condition is met
Progressive danger state; when depleted fully, player reset to stage start

danger_signals.visual_form: Large enemy entities at end of stages
danger_signals.meaning: Critical threat; major challenge requiring weapons/items to defeat

safety_signals.meaning: Beneficial pickups; S increases mobility, P increases firing power, B gives bonus points

safety_signals.visual_form: Flash Buster, Megasmasher, Mega Buster variants
safety_signals.meaning: Enhanced offensive capability; progression through weapon tiers
Temporary invulnerability; absorbs 32 shots before depleting
Safe passage; allows navigation to different sections

safety_signals.visual_form: Mega Missile, Flash Bomb, Crush Ball items
safety_signals.meaning: Tactical advantage; activated by pressing both buttons simultaneously at cost of 4 energy points

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Laser rifle and weapon upgrades; appears within space station corridors

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: L Brain final boss; various alien creature designs
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy types throughout space station; boss entities at stage conclusions
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Space station structure itself as base of operations; alien architecture; dome-shaped power generators

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Section numbering (00-19, 20-39, 40-59); environmental design changes between sections
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Difficulty escalation; new enemy types; boss patterns intensify across stages

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Generators present in specific sections; likely visually distinct mechanical structures
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Blocking access to final section; must be destroyed to open path; destroying them increases max energy
Path to final boss section (19, 39, 59) opens; player energy capacity increases
Higher death risk; one-hit death without energy buffer; visual tension indicator
Automatic stage reset; player returns to beginning of current stage with full energy
Gameplay progression from outside to inside space station; narrative space infiltration

environmental_storytelling.technique: Space station architecture as faction identity
environmental_storytelling.example: Alien-designed corridors, sections labeled numerically, dome-shaped power generators reflect Balangool technological aesthetic
Two transporter choices per section create branching paths; some transporters blocked until conditions met—implies base control systems and locked areas
Destruction of power generators per stage communicates sabotage mission structure; final boss 'L Brain' implies central command entity
Starting at Section 00 (outside), progressing inward to Section 01+ communicates deep infiltration of enemy base; spatial progression mirrors narrative progress

environmental_storytelling.technique: Energy system as resource scarcity worldbuilding
environmental_storytelling.example: Gradual energy depletion from combat and damage reflects hostile environment; energy recovery through safe item pickups implies resource management tension

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Fire projectiles at player; varied movement patterns; present threat throughout sections
Appear at end of each stage; employ distinct attack patterns; require upgraded weapons to defeat efficiently
Block access to final stage section; remain stationary targets; can be destroyed by player fire
Cannot be entered; kill player on contact; become accessible after specific condition fulfillment
Full mobility; can sustain multiple hits; can engage enemies aggressively
Increased vulnerability; one-hit-death risk; behavioral necessity for caution and avoidance

notes: Section Z NES uses spatial infiltration narrative (astronaut destroying alien space station). Danger/safety signals rely heavily on resource management (energy depletion mechanic) and enemy encounter design rather than environmental hazards. World-state communication is minimal in available sources but inferred from gameplay structure (stage progression, power generator objectives, transporter logic). Faction distinction is asymmetric: player is solo infiltrator vs. distributed Balangool empire (multiple enemy types, centralized L Brain boss). Visual communication appears sprite-based and minimal per NES technical constraints; meaning conveyed through mechanic-to-visual mapping (generator destruction = path opening) rather than detailed environmental storytelling.
